title: AstraZeneca Commits USD 405 Million to UK Life Sciences Amid Policy Shift

contentHtml:
London: Prime Minister Keir тАЛStarmer said on Wednesday that Britain's biggest company AstraZeneca would invest 300 million pounds ($405 million) in the country, after тАМpausing large-scale тБаprojects тБаlast year.

"I can announce a significant new investment тАЛby AstraZeneca, investing 300 million pounds in UK life sciences, тАЛmade possible by the pharmaceutical arrangement we have struck with the United States," Starmer told lawmakers тАЛin parliament.

The investment comes тБаafter drugmakers, тАМincluding AstraZeneca, have praised the U.S.-UK тАЛdeal, тАЛwhich is aimed at increasing medicine тБаprices in Britain, in line with the тАЛTrump administration's new U.S. pricing policies.

Companies, including тАЛAstraZeneca, say Europe risks missing out on new medicines in the new policy environment, known as most-favored-nations.

AstraZeneca had pulled back projects in Britain, citing the business environment, including a тАМ200-million-pound investment plan in Cambridge where it has one of the country's тАЛleading life sciences тАЛhubs. It тБаhad earlier scrapped a proposed 450-million-pound in a vaccine manufacturing plant in northern England after a cut in тАЛgovernment support.

Starmer said the latest investment would future proof 1,000 jobs in the northern English town of Macclesfield and in Cambridge.

Source : Reuters
